description: This API executes the fourth release of the IEC short circuit calculations function. This API is used to apply a series of faults at various locations in the working case to compute short circuit currents according to IEC 60909 standard. This API routine was first introduced in release 33.0.0. ierr = iecs_4(sid, all, status, values, iecfile, fcdfile, scfile)
python_syntax: ierr = iecs_4(sid, all, status, values, iecfile, fcdfile, scfile)

parameters.name: sid
parameters.description: A valid subsystem identifier. valid subsystem identifiers range from 0 to 11. subsystem sid must have been previously defined. (input; 0 by default).

parameters.name: all
parameters.description: All buses or specified subsystem flag. (input; 1 by default).

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: An array of seventeen elements specifying integer options. (input).

parameters.name: values
parameters.description: An array of two elements specifying real values. (input).

parameters.name: iecfile
parameters.description: Iec fault calculation data (.iec) file. (input; blank by default).

parameters.name: fcdfile
parameters.description: Fault control (.fcd) file. (input; blank by default).

all.description: process only buses in subsystem SID.

status.description: three phase faults (0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘flt3ph’.
omit.
include.
line-to-ground faults (0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘fltlg’.
omit.
include.
line-line-to-ground faults (0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘fltllg’.
omit.
include.
line-to-line faults (0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘fltll’.
omit.
include.
report option (1 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘rptop’.
fault current summary table.
total fault currents.
fault contributions to N levels away.
total fault currents and fault contributions to N levels away.
number of levels back for contributions (0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘rptlvl’.
used if STATUS(5) = 2 or 3.
location of fault (0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘fltloc’.
fault at network bus.
fault at LV bus of Power Station Unit (PSU).
fault at auxiliary transformer (connected to PSU) LV bus.
line-out faults (0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘linout’.
omit.
include.
line-end faults (0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘linend’.
omit.
include.
transformer tap ratios and phase shift angles option (0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘tpunty’.
leave tap ratios and phase shift angles unchanged.
set tap ratios to 1.0 pu and phase shift angles to 0 deg.
set tap ratios to 1.0 pu and phase shift angles unchanged.
set tap ratios unchanged and phase shift angles to 0 deg.
line charging option (1 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘lnchrg’.
leave unchanged.
set to 0.0 in the positive and negative sequences.
set to 0.0 in all sequences.
line, fixed and switched shunts, and magnetizing admittance option (1 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘shntop’.
leave unchanged.
set to 0.0 in the positive and negative sequences.
set to 0.0 in all sequences.
dc line and FACTS device option (0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘dcload’.
blocked.
represent as load.
zero sequence transformer impedance correction option (0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘zcorec’.
ignore.
apply.
option for Voltage Factor C (0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘cfactor’.
Voltage Factor C for maximum fault currents.
Voltage Factor C for minimum fault currents.
Voltage Factor C as specified by ‘ucfactor’ value for maximum fault current calculations.
Voltage Factor C as specified by ‘ucfactor’ value for minimum fault current calculations.
load option (1 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘loadop’.
leave unchanged.
set to 0.0 in the positive and negative sequences.
set to 0.0 in all sequences.
fault analysis generator reactance option; ignored if generators are already “converted” (0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘genxop’.
use subtransient reactance.
use transient reactance.
use synchronous reactance.

values.description: breaker contact parting time in seconds (5/base Frequency by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘brktim’.
user specified voltage factor c (1.0 by default) This is used only when STATUS(15)=2 or 3), can also be specified with keyword ‘ucfactor’.

error_codes.description: invalid SID value or subsystem SID is not defined.
prerequisite requirements for API are not met.
three winding transformer SC results not available.
Fault analysis invalid, in-service induction machines stalled or tripped state.
Line end fault invalid–bus table is full.
SC result file size exceeded. Define smaller size bus subsystems and re-run if SCFILE is required.
topology error - bus(es) with bus type code and series element status inconsistencies.
Number of branches at faulted bus are greater than NBRLINKS (only arrbox.fault module).
Re-run by specifying higher NBRLNKS value (only arrbox.fault module).
Network case has number of MOV and/or NCS, cannot use arrbox.iecs_currents, use arrbox.fault module.
